surface.name: Node.js CommonJS Error Monitoring candidate
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Node.js CommonJS Error Monitoring candidate. Node.js is a fully documented SDK family and a useful server-side candidate, but Sentry does not rank it above the other SDKs. The current CommonJS verification instructions are preserved exactly, including their unresolved binding gap. Prior surface before policy commit: Sentry account and project setup, with a Node.js CommonJS error-monitoring candidate.

alternatives_considered.name: Other Sentry SDK quick starts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The official SDK index lists many first-class platform families with different packages, files, credentials, runtime requirements, and verification actions, without selecting one canonical first route.

alternatives_considered.name: Sentry API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The API manages Sentry resources, while SDK quick starts are the documented routes for sending application telemetry.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Sentry account and project, select the application's SDK, instrument it, and verify the first captured issue.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Sentry, but has not supplied a language or framework.
The developer can choose an immutable US or EU data-storage location for the new organization.
For the recorded candidate only, the developer has an already-running CommonJS Node.js application on Node.js 18.0.0 or later.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: A new error appears in Issues and opens to show full details and context.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A new error appears in Issues and opens to show full details and context.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: A new error appears in the Issues stream and opens to show its full details and context.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: A new error appears in Issues and opens to show full details and context.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; the terminal is the Node.js SDK guide's documented first error appearing in Issues. The SDK-choice and CommonJS binding caveats are recorded in uncertainties.

prerequisites.requirement: A name, organization name, email address, and password, or an identity accepted by Google, GitHub, or Azure DevOps signup.
A decision between US and EU Data Storage Location. Sentry says it cannot be changed after the organization is created; switching later requires a new organization.
Permission to create a Sentry project. If member project creation is disabled, the project-creation API requires org:write scope.
For the Node.js candidate, an application already running on Node.js 18.0.0 or later, with npm and a writable CommonJS project.

primary_path.action: Open Sentry's Create your free account page.
details: The page advertises unlimited volume and every feature for 14 days, then automatic movement to the Free plan with no forced upgrade.
primary_path.success_signal: Fields for Name, Organization, Email, Password, and Data Storage Location are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The signup page states that JavaScript must be enabled.

primary_path.action: Enter Name, Organization, Email, and Password.
details: Google, GitHub, and Azure DevOps are documented alternative signup providers.
primary_path.success_signal: The required identity fields are accepted.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid, missing, or already-associated values can prevent submission; the public page does not enumerate every validation message.

primary_path.action: Choose US or EU as the organization's Data Storage Location.
details: US data is physically stored in Iowa and EU data in Frankfurt.
The selection applies to at-rest customer data; Sentry documents exceptions for certain global metadata and services.
primary_path.success_signal: Exactly one storage location is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Sentry says this value cannot be changed after organization creation. A later switch requires creating a new organization.

primary_path.action: Choose whether to receive occasional updates, accept the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, complete reCAPTCHA, and select Get set up.
primary_path.success_signal: Sentry accepts the signup and enters the organization setup experience.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Terms acceptance and reCAPTCHA are required; anti-abuse or form validation can block submission.

primary_path.action: Sign in if necessary, open Projects in the left navigation, and select Create Project.
primary_path.success_signal: The platform, alert-frequency, project-name, and team controls are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Organization policy may disable member project creation; an authorized user with org:write scope must create the project or change the policy.

primary_path.action: Choose the application's platform and continue on that platform-specific setup route.
details: The project guide demonstrates React as an example, while the platform index lists Android, Apple, Dart, Elixir, Go, Godot, Java, JavaScript, Kotlin, Native, .NET, Nintendo Switch, PHP, PlayStation, PowerShell, Python, React Native, Ruby, Rust, Unity, Unreal, Xbox, and community SDKs.
primary_path.success_signal: A platform-specific setup flow is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Sentry does not designate a canonical platform. The journey cannot continue reproducibly until the target application platform is known.

primary_path.action: On Create Project, choose Node.js as the application platform.
details: The generic project guide says to select the platform based on the application's code.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting another platform changes the entire SDK candidate.

primary_path.action: Choose the alert frequency, enter a project name, assign an existing team or create a new team, then select Create Project.
details: The project guide demonstrates Alert me on high priority issues as its alert-frequency selection.
primary_path.input: Alert preference, project name, and team
primary_path.success_signal: Sentry displays the new project's setup instructions and unique DSN.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing required values, naming conflicts, team access, or the member-creation policy can block creation.

primary_path.action: Copy the project's unique DSN, then select Take me to Issues.
details: The DSN is the project-specific value used by SDK initialization.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer has the DSN and can view the project's empty or existing Issues list.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Losing or substituting the DSN can send no events or send them to the wrong project.

primary_path.action: Confirm the application is already running on Node.js 18.0.0 or later and uses CommonJS for this candidate.
details: Node.js 18.19.0 or 19.9.0 and later is recommended for full TracingChannel support.
primary_path.success_signal: The application runs on a documented Node.js version and can load CommonJS modules.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An older runtime requires an upgrade or a separately verified compatible SDK version; ESM uses a different loading route.

primary_path.action: In the interactive quick start, retain Issues and do not enable optional Logs, Tracing, or Profiling for the minimal error-monitoring candidate.
details: Issues are always enabled; optional toggles change packages, initialization, and verification.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide shows the @sentry/node install and Issues verification instructions.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Enabling other features expands the setup and terminal checks.

primary_path.action: From the application directory, run npm install @sentry/node.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package-manager, network, lockfile, permission, or compatibility failures block setup.

primary_path.action: Create instrument.js in the project root.
primary_path.success_signal: instrument.js exists where the later require path resolves it.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different location requires a matching require path.

primary_path.action: In instrument.js, bind const Sentry = require('@sentry/node') and call Sentry.init with the project DSN and sendDefaultPii: true, plus only configuration produced by the selected feature toggles.
details: sendDefaultPii: true appears inside the current official initialization example; it is not documented as a separate selector in this route.
primary_path.success_signal: instrument.js contains the documented require and init call.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or wrong DSN prevents delivery to the intended project. Enabling default PII has privacy implications that the developer must evaluate.

primary_path.action: Add require('./instrument') at the very beginning of app.js, before requiring any other modules.
details: The documented statement loads instrument.js for side effects but does not assign its local Sentry binding in app.js.
primary_path.success_signal: require('./instrument') is the first module load.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Loading it later can prevent automatic instrumentation from working correctly.

primary_path.action: Skip the optional source-map wizard and proceed to Verify Your Setup.
details: Source maps improve stack-trace readability but are explicitly optional.
primary_path.success_signal: The Issues verification snippet is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The eventual stack can be less readable without source maps.

primary_path.action: Attempt to add the official Issues verification snippet in app.js, which waits 99 milliseconds, calls undefined foo(), catches the exception, and calls Sentry.captureException(e).
details: The preceding CommonJS steps bind Sentry only as a local constant inside instrument.js.
require('./instrument') in app.js does not assign a Sentry identifier and the documented instrument.js example does not export one.
primary_path.input: Official verification snippet and documented CommonJS setup
primary_path.success_signal: No documented success signal is reachable at this step because Sentry is not in scope in app.js.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Executing the snippet as documented reaches Sentry.captureException with no documented Sentry binding in app.js. Additional official evidence or a corrected guide is required; this record does not invent a binding.

primary_path.action: After the binding gap is resolved by an officially documented method, start or restart the app, wait a couple of moments, open Issues, select the new error, and inspect its details and context.
details: This is the guide's intended downstream terminal, not a completed step in the currently documented CommonJS sequence.
primary_path.input: A valid captured exception, which the preceding official CommonJS sequence does not currently produce
primary_path.success_signal: A new error appears in Issues and opens to show full details and context.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This step remains unreachable from the preceding documented steps. Once reachable, the guide says captured data can take a couple of moments to appear.

branches.path: Choose Google, GitHub, or Azure DevOps instead of entering the password-based form, then complete the provider flow and return to Sentry setup.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the direct identity-entry action with an external identity-provider handoff.

branches.condition: The organization later needs the other Data Storage Location.
branches.path: Create a new organization in the desired region; the existing organization's region cannot be changed.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Forces a new organization journey and migration rather than an in-place correction.

branches.path: Ask a user authorized with org:write to create the project or change the organization policy, then return to the SDK setup.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an administrator or elevated-permission dependency before a DSN exists.

branches.condition: The application is not Node.js CommonJS.
branches.path: Choose the actual language and framework from the SDK index and follow its own complete quick start.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces every candidate-specific package, file, initialization, execution, and verification action.

branches.path: Use instrument.mjs and the documented node --import ./instrument.mjs app.mjs route, with Node.js 18.19.0 or later for --import.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the CommonJS loading action and may avoid or alter the CommonJS-specific binding inconsistency, but requires its own end-to-end validation.

branches.condition: Logs, Tracing, Profiling, or readable source maps are required in the first session.
branches.path: Enable the relevant quick-start toggles and follow the generated extra package, initialization, test-code, wizard, and product-page verification instructions.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Expands the candidate beyond the minimal Issues route and can introduce additional terminals.

friction_gates.description: The data-storage region is immutable for the organization.
Terms acceptance and reCAPTCHA must be completed before signup submission.
Organization policy can prevent members from creating the required project; the elevated API scope is org:write.
Sentry requires an SDK platform choice and does not document one platform-wide default.
The recorded candidate requires an existing supported Node.js application and changes by module system and selected features.
The current CommonJS quick-start verification references Sentry in app.js without documenting a binding there.

sources.title: Create your free account
evidence_supported: Google, GitHub, and Azure DevOps alternatives

sources.title: Create a Project for an Organization
sections_used: Create a Project for an Organization

uncertainties.question: Which SDK should represent Sentry in the cross-platform comparison?
uncertainties.impact: The prerequisites, package, runtime, files, credential placement, event trigger, waits, and terminal page all change by SDK.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official platform index presents many co-equal SDK routes and does not establish a canonical first SDK.

uncertainties.question: What official CommonJS action makes Sentry available to the verification snippet in app.js?
uncertainties.impact: Without a documented binding, the candidate cannot execute Sentry.captureException or reach its first issue.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current guide binds Sentry locally in instrument.js, then only requires that file for side effects in app.js before showing a snippet that calls Sentry in app.js. No export, assignment, or second binding is documented in the sequence.

excluded_after_success: Source-map upload in the minimal candidate
Non-Node SDK journeys pending human selection
